pi_items.source: install_panoply.sh download location and setup_task.sh file check
Python netCDF4 inspection of the downloaded NOAA NCEP dataset
setup_task.sh directory creation and export_result.sh checks
export_result.sh expects these exact file paths for evaluation

Python analysis of the NCEP prate netCDF file, confirming maximum precipitation in the Eastern Pacific (longitudes 200-280) remains in the Northern Hemisphere (~8.5N) for both January and July, which aligns with the well-documented climatological absence of an equator-crossing ITCZ in this region.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis monthly precipitation data (prate) downloaded from NOAA. Analysis of the downloaded netCDF file confirms that the Intertropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ) over the Eastern Pacific (longitudes 200-280) does not cross the equator; the maximum precipitation band stays in the Northern Hemisphere (around 8.5°N) in both January and July. The required output directory and file paths are correctly enforced by the export scripts.
